commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is needed; maintainers should decide the narrow message-delivery fallback risk and wait for exact-head checks before merge.; Cleared: The diff only changes agent delivery fallback logic and colocated tests; it does not touch dependencies, workflows, secrets, publishing, or other supply-chain surfaces. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a narrow generated-media direct fallback when active requester wake has already failed and the requester-agent handoff throws a session write-lock-shaped error, plus regression coverage for the lock and generic-error branches.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +103. Total +125 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main rethrows the requester-agent error after active wake failure, while the PR's targeted regression injects the session write-lock shape and exercises the fallback branch. I did not run a local reproduction because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; maintainers should decide the narrow message-delivery fallback risk and wait for exact-head checks before merge.

target.title: fix: fallback when generated media handoff locks
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep the existing #85489 active-wake generated-media fallback intact.
- Narrow the thrown-error fallback to the session write-lock/no-response shape only.
- Preserve the visible failure contract for generic requester-agent handoff errors so they do not direct-send generated media after an unknown side effect.

## Why this is narrow

This does not change the normal active-session success path, dormant requester behavior, or generic requester-agent handoff failures. The fallback is only attempted when all of these are true:

- the completion is for an agent-mediated generated-media tool,
- expected media URLs are present,
- the active requester wake has already failed,
- the requester-agent handoff throws before producing a response,
- the thrown error is recognized as `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError`, `OPENCLAW_SESSION_WRITE_LOCK_TIMEOUT`, or the serialized `session file locked` message,
- the existing direct media delivery target is available.

This specifically covers the observed Spartacus failure shape: generation succeeded, active wake failed with `source_reply_delivery_mode_mismatch`, the requester-agent handoff then hit a session write lock, and the generated image was reported as failed instead of being delivered.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: active wake failure followed by a requester-agent session write-lock handoff error now delivers the generated media through the existing idempotent `generated-media-direct` fallback. Generic requester-agent handoff errors still return a visible failed delivery record and do not direct-send media.

Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox, Linux CI-like runner, synced PR checkout at `dac46ed3610b6d9725ade64cf4e168a3450d3d92`. The run covered both the exact lock-shaped fallback under the production `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` path and a real qa-channel generated-media smoke with the mock OpenAI provider.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run \
  --provider blacksmith-testbox \
  --blacksmith-org openclaw \
  --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml \
  --blacksmith-job check \
  --blacksmith-ref main \
  --idle-timeout 90m \
  --ttl 240m \
  --timing-json \
  --shell -- \
  'set -euo pipefail
   echo "CRABBOX_PHASE:exact-lock-fallback"
   node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "directly delivers generated media after active wake failure when requester handoff locks|keeps generic requester handoff errors visible after active wake failure"
   echo "CRABBOX_PHASE:qa-channel-generated-media"
   pnpm openclaw qa suite --provider-mode mock-openai --scenario native-image-generation --concurrency 1 --output-dir .artifacts/qa-e2e/pr-87741-crabbox-native-image-generation
  '
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
provider=blacksmith-testbox id=tbx_01ksr2jtt3fnz0zqvwmqq513h7 sync=delegated auth=blacksmith
GitHub Actions run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26598739064
CRABBOX_PHASE:exact-lock-fallback
✓ agents-core ../../src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts > deliverSubagentAnnouncement completion delivery > directly delivers generated media after active wake failure when requester handoff locks
✓ agents-core ../../src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts > deliverSubagentAnnouncement completion delivery > keeps generic requester handoff errors visible after active wake failure
✓ agents-support ../../src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts > deliverSubagentAnnouncement completion delivery > directly delivers generated media after active wake failure when requester handoff locks
✓ agents-support ../../src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts > deliverSubagentAnnouncement completion delivery > keeps generic requester handoff errors visible after active wake failure
Test Files 2 passed (2)
Tests 4 passed | 148 skipped (152)
CRABBOX_PHASE:qa-channel-generated-media
QA suite report: /home/runner/_work/openclaw/openclaw/.artifacts/qa-e2e/pr-87741-crabbox-native-image-generation/qa-suite-report.md
QA suite summary: /home/runner/_work/openclaw/openclaw/.artifacts/qa-e2e/pr-87741-crabbox-native-image-generation/qa-suite-summary.json
blacksmith run summary sync=delegated command=2m30.811s total=2m37.477s exit=0
{"provider":"blacksmith-testbox","leaseId":"tbx_01ksr2jtt3fnz0zqvwmqq513h7","slug":"violet-krill","syncDelegated":true,"commandPhases":[{"name":"user-command","ms":36169},{"name":"exact-lock-fallback","ms":75931},{"name":"qa-channel-generated-media","ms":38710}],"exitCode":0}
Testbox tbx_01ksr2jtt3fnz0zqvwmqq513h7 stopped (completed)
```

Observed result after fix: the exact lock-shaped handoff case sends exactly `/tmp/generated-locked.png` with idempotency key `announce-channel-media-handoff-locked:generated-media-direct`; the generic-error case returns `delivered: false`, `path: direct`, and does not call `sendMessage`. The qa-channel generated-media scenario also completed successfully in the same remote Testbox run, proving the generated-media tool path still produces a saved media artifact through the real QA gateway/channel smoke.

What was not tested: forcing the nondeterministic production session-file lock inside a live long-running qa-channel turn. The exact lock shape is injected at the gateway handoff boundary in the remote regression, and the real qa-channel generated-media delivery path is covered as a separate smoke in the same Crabbox run.

## Verification

- Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox: `tbx_01ksr2jtt3fnz0zqvwmqq513h7` / `violet-krill`, provider `blacksmith-testbox`, synced checkout, exit 0. Actions run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26598739064.
- Remote exact fallback phase: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "directly delivers generated media after active wake failure when requester handoff locks|keeps generic requester handoff errors visible after active wake failure"` -> passed, `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 4 passed | 148 skipped (152)`.
- Remote qa-channel generated-media smoke: `pnpm openclaw qa suite --provider-mode mock-openai --scenario native-image-generation --concurrency 1 --output-dir .artifacts/qa-e2e/pr-87741-crabbox-native-image-generation` -> passed, QA summary/report written in the remote artifact directory.
- Local focused regression: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts --reporter=verbose` -> passed, `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 152 passed (152)`.
- `pnpm format:fix -- src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` -> passed.
- `git diff --check` -> passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings.


- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts --reporter=verbose` -> passed, `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 152 passed (152)`.
- `pnpm format:fix -- src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` -> passed.
- `git diff --check` -> passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Known baseline issue

- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH pnpm tsgo:core` previously failed on unchanged upstream/main code:

```text
src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.abort-settle-timeout.ts(4,3): error TS2322: Type 'ProcessEnv' is not assignable to type 'Pick<ProcessEnv, "OPENCLAW_EMBEDDED_ABORT_SETTLE_TIMEOUT_MS" | "OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST">'.
```

That file is untouched by this PR; the PR diff is limited to `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts` and `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts`.
